content: The open-ended, incremental search for high-reward novelty that distinguishes ICM, RND, and other curiosity-driven RL methods from purpose-built curious agents can be reframed as an emergent property of agent memory scaling in 2024-2025 SOTA active learning agents. Instead of viewing intrinsic curiosity modules as add-on components to a base agent, the intrinsic drive to explore unexpected state spaces arises from mismatches between stored memory and new incoming observations, just as ICM uses prediction error to generate curiosity reward.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
